label: Industry analysis / trend piece
opening_rule: First sentence: the specific claim being made about where the industry is heading -- not background, not 'the industry is changing'.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: name the specific companies, platforms, or audiences that win and lose if this trend continues. No 'only time will tell'. No 'the future remains uncertain'.

sections.name: The trend
sections.purpose: What is actually happening -- with data, named examples, and a timeframe. Not 'there is a growing interest in X' but 'X has grown from Y to Z since when, driven by these specific forces'.

sections.name: Why now
sections.purpose: What changed to make this trend possible or inevitable. Name the specific technological, economic, or cultural shifts. Avoid post-hoc rationalization dressed as analysis.

sections.name: Who is driving it
sections.purpose: Name the specific companies, creators, or communities pushing this trend. What are their incentives? Are they aligned with user interests or against them?

sections.name: Who gets hurt
sections.purpose: Every trend has losers. Name them specifically -- companies, job categories, user groups. Do not sanitize this with 'challenges ahead'.

sections.name: Verdict
sections.purpose: Is this trend good, bad, or inevitable regardless? Take a position. Name the specific outcome you expect in 2-3 years and why. Do not restate the trend. End on the consequence that most people are not yet talking about.
